title: Lab 22: Container Autostart With Systemd
description: Run a rootless container as a persistent user service.

tasks.title: Run The Container
tasks.prompt: Create user merin22 with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. Then, as that user, run a container named render22 from localhost/fluxpdf22:latest with /opt/inbox22 mounted to /data/input and /opt/outbox22 mounted to /data/output.

tasks.title: Generate User Service
tasks.prompt: As user merin22, generate a systemd user unit for that container and enable it.
solution_commands: runuser -l merin22 -c "cd ~/.config/systemd/user && podman generate systemd --name render22 --files --new"

tasks.title: Enable Lingering
tasks.prompt: Enable lingering for merin22 so the user service starts automatically after reboot.

hints: Use podman generate systemd --files --new or podman generate systemd --new depending on the environment.
The user service must run without an active login session.

checks.original_command: runuser -l merin22 -c 'systemctl --user is-enabled container-render22.service' | grep -qx enabled
checks.command: runuser -l merin22 -c 'systemctl --user is-enabled container-render22.service' | grep -qx enabled

checks.original_command: runuser -l merin22 -c 'systemctl --user is-active container-render22.service' | grep -qx active
checks.command: runuser -l merin22 -c 'systemctl --user is-active container-render22.service' | grep -qx active
